# UC-11.2.3 · Drive Sharing Anomalies

## Description

Unusual file sharing patterns may indicate data exfiltration or accidental exposure of sensitive documents.

## Value

Unusual file sharing patterns may indicate data exfiltration or accidental exposure of sensitive documents.

## Implementation

Ingest Drive audit logs. Alert on external sharing, "anyone with link" sharing, and bulk sharing events. Track sharing patterns per user. Flag sharing of sensitive folders or documents.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Google Drive audit log.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest Drive audit logs. Alert on external sharing, "anyone with link" sharing, and bulk sharing events. Track sharing patterns per user. Flag sharing of sensitive folders or documents.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:drive" event_name="change_user_access"
| where new_value="people_with_link" OR target_user_email NOT LIKE "%@yourdomain.com%"
| table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value
```

Understanding this SPL

**Drive Sharing Anomalies** — Unusual file sharing patterns may indicate data exfiltration or accidental exposure of sensitive documents.

Documented **Data sources**: Google Drive audit log. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:drive. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:drive". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where new_value="people_with_link" OR target_user_email NOT LIKE "%@yourdomain.com%"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Drive Sharing Anomalies**): table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sharing events), Bar chart (external sharing by user), Line chart (sharing activity trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:drive" event_name="change_user_access"
| where new_value="people_with_link" OR target_user_email NOT LIKE "%@yourdomain.com%"
| table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value
```

## Visualization

Table (sharing events), Bar chart (external sharing by user), Line chart (sharing activity trend).
